title: orobanche uniflora var. purpurea
intro: Orobanche uniflora var. purpurea is a rare, parasitic flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region, belonging to the broomrape family (Orobanchaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The plant has been traditionally used to treat respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs, and its health benefits also include its potential to reduce fever and alleviate digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its availability is limited due to its rarity and the need for a host plant to grow.
